hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 38th percentile; overheal (69.6%) exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse (59.5%) by 10.1 points, and HPM (3.11) ran at 0.53x the sample average. 15 deaths this kill - unusually high for Hydross, well above the 0 deaths Vinnyvozz's other two analyzed Hydross kills recorded.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom (30.1%) undershot the sample's 41.0% share, the largest gap this kill; Swiftmend (14.1%) and the Bloom-rank Lifebloom (8.6%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (7.1% and 4.5%).
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed this pipeline's deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self), and Swiftmend was cast 5 times (Luffydonoo, Rolopally x2, Captinhelp, Leechers), close to the sample's own average. With 15 real deaths this kill, none lines up cleanly with a single missed cooldown - they cluster in two waves (roughly 4-19 seconds after one Swiftmend cast, then 5-17 seconds after another), reading as sustained raid damage outpacing the available healing rather than any one missed save.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (80.5%) landed in line with the sample average (83.5%), but the top target (Luffydonoo, 25.3%) took a notably smaller share than the sample's typical top-target concentration (48.5%) - healing was spread wider at the top than usual, consistent with a kill that had many different people taking damage.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 3rd percentile, by far the weakest kill of this raid, and driven by one clear real event: Vinnyvozz died 105 seconds into this 334-second kill (31.5% of the way through), and active time (31.5%), Tree of Life uptime (31.5%), and HPS (247, just 0.15x the sample average) all reflect that same cutoff almost exactly. Overheal (62.9%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (49.1%) by 13.8 points even in that shortened window.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (22.0%) undershot the sample's 39.2% share; Swiftmend (14.9%) and Lifebloom (37.1%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (4.7% and 28.4%) - though composition numbers here reflect roughly a third of a normal kill's healing window, not a full-length comparison.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate and Nature's Swiftness both went unused against real sample usage rates of 90% and 75% - though with only ~105 seconds of real playtime this kill, there was limited window to use either regardless. Swiftmend was cast 4 times (Fuggler, Hezrun, Vajomee, Firewoody) before the death. The four deaths that follow 146-184 seconds after Vinnyvozz's last real cooldown cast (Calamety, Braila, Victim, Ghostpepperx) all happened after Vinnyvozz herself was already dead, not because of any missed cooldown - the two deaths right around her own (Staubaoz at the same timestamp) look like the same damage event that killed her.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (59.2%) and top-target concentration (18.0%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample - not a meaningful comparison given how much of this kill Vinnyvozz missed after dying early.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 31st percentile; overheal (62.0%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (54.3%) by 7.7 points, and HPM (3.45) ran at 0.54x the sample average. 10 deaths this kill, though none of them Vinnyvozz's own.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (32.9%) undershot the sample's 52.8% share, the largest gap this kill; Rejuvenation (27.0%) and Swiftmend (16.8%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (13.3% and 4.7%) to compensate.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed the deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self), and Swiftmend was cast 8 times across 7 targets, close to the sample's own average. Of the 10 deaths, the closest pairs (Megatauro 0.8s after a Mana Potion cast, Leechers 0.4s after a Swiftmend cast) are too close to the wrong kind of cooldown - Mana Potion doesn't heal, and the Swiftmend cast nearest Leechers wasn't necessarily aimed there - to read as clean missed saves rather than coincidental timing during a busy kill.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (63.0%) ran above the sample average (51.4%); top-target concentration (19.4%, Megatauro) landed in line with the sample's typical 15.7%.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 17th percentile, the second-weakest kill of this raid; overheal (62.6%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (46.5%) by 16.1 points - the widest gap of this raid - and HPM (4.01) ran at 0.49x the sample average. Active time (99.8%) was in line, and 1 death this kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom (22.7%) undershot the sample's 40.2% share, the largest gap this kill; Rejuvenation (23.0%) ran ahead of the sample's 12.7% to compensate.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed the deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self), and Swiftmend was cast 5 times (Braila, Captinhelp, Dormammoo, Silkyrebel, Hezrun), close to the sample's own average. The one death this kill (Megatauro) follows a Swiftmend cast by just 0.9 seconds, but Megatauro isn't among that cast's listed targets, so it reads as damage that outpaced a heal aimed elsewhere rather than a clean missed save.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Both target coverage (56.1% vs. a 74.9% sample average) and top-target concentration (19.7%, Captinhelp, vs. a 34.9% sample average) ran well below the sample - a real, wide-spread kill with no single dominant recipient.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: 47th percentile, the best relative kill of this raid; overheal (63.1%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (51.1%) by 12.0 points, and HPM (3.98) ran at 0.56x the sample average. Food was not active this kill, the one consumable gap this raid.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom (27.2%) undershot the sample's 37.4% share, the largest gap this kill. Tranquility shows 0 casts in the cooldown table below, but the real healing data shows it was genuinely cast once (7.7% of healing, guid 44208) - this class's Tranquility guid isn't wired into the cooldown-tracking table yet, so the table undercounts it rather than showing a real absence.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth was cast once this kill, targeting Porgrok, who had died 1.8 seconds earlier - a real, correctly-timed battle-rez. It's flagged as a real deviation ("overcast") only because just 17% of the Top 100 sample uses Rebirth at all on this specific boss - a rare-but-appropriate use, not a mistake. Everything else (Innervate, Nature's Swiftness, Swiftmend x6) landed in line with the sample. Of the other 2 deaths, neither lines up closely enough with a cooldown cast to read as a missed save.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Both target coverage (47.0% vs. a 67.4% sample average) and top-target concentration (20.5%, Captinhelp, vs. a 38.8% sample average) ran below the sample - the widest, most spread-out healing pattern of this raid.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 19th percentile, the third-weakest kill of this raid; overheal (67.2%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (53.5%) by 13.7 points, and HPM (3.81) ran at 0.55x the sample average. Active time (98.1%) was in line, and 6 deaths this kill.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (9.5%) undershot the sample's 21.9% share, the largest gap this kill; Swiftmend (9.4%) and Lifebloom (50.8%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (4.4% and 46.7%).
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 86% sample usage rate - the only real cooldown deviation this kill. Swiftmend was cast 8 times across 7 targets, close to the sample's own average. Of the 6 deaths, all trail the nearest Swiftmend cast by 6.9-50.4 seconds and none were that cast's real target - none reads as a clean missed save.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (49.9%) and top-target concentration (19.2%, Braila) both landed in line with the sample (50.9% and 21.0%).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: 30th percentile; overheal (68.6%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (61.1%) by 7.5 points, and HPM (3.21) ran at 0.51x the sample average. Active time (98.4%) was in line, and 4 deaths this kill.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (9.5%) undershot the sample's 20.9% share, the largest gap this kill; Lifebloom (55.3%) ran ahead of the sample's 49.4%.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: No cooldowns crossed the deviation threshold - Innervate and Nature's Swiftness were each cast once (self). Rebirth was cast once, targeting Rolopally, who had died 13.8 seconds earlier - a real, if slightly delayed, battle-rez, and in line with a real 29% sample usage rate on this boss. Swiftmend was cast 8 times across 8 targets, close to the sample's own average. Of the other deaths, none lines up closely enough with a cooldown to read as a clean missed save.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (79.8%) and top-target concentration (37.1%, Rolopally) both landed in line with the sample (77.9% and 42.7%).

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 63rd percentile, the best kill of this raid; overheal (39.4%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (33.0%) by 6.4 points, the narrowest gap this raid, even at this strong percentile. HPM (7.0) ran at 0.84x the sample average, the closest to sample-normal of any kill this raid. Active time (99.7%) was in line, and 2 deaths this kill.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (28.5%) undershot the sample's 76.6% share by the widest margin of any kill this raid; both Lifebloom ranks ran far ahead of their sample shares instead (HoT rank 40.6% vs. 4.9%, Bloom rank 23.2% vs. 2.6%) - close to an inverted version of the sample's usual Regrowth-heavy composition, the same pattern seen on this boss in Vinnyvozz's other analyzed raid night.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against the lowest sample-usage rate of any kill this raid (55%) but still crossed this pipeline's undercast threshold. Everything else - Innervate, Swiftmend, Mana Potion - landed in line with the sample. The 2 deaths this kill both trail the single Swiftmend cast by 29-37 seconds, too far to read as related.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (62.4%) ran well above the sample average (37.2%); the top target was Vinnyvozz's own name (17.2% self-healing), in line with the sample's typical top-target concentration (9.4%).

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: 42nd percentile; overheal (77.3%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (65.4%) by 11.9 points, and HPM (2.43) ran at 0.5x the sample average. Active time (98.8%) was in line, and no deaths this kill - the only deathless kill of this raid.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (22.6%) undershot the sample's 59.4% share by the widest margin of any kill this raid; Lifebloom (38.6%) and Swiftmend (17.2%) both ran ahead of their sample shares (13.1% and 6.7%) to compensate.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate went unused against an 82% sample usage rate - the only cooldown that crossed this pipeline's deviation threshold on this kill, and a different cooldown than the one that recurred most often elsewhere this raid (Nature's Swiftness, which was actually cast here). Everything else landed in line with the sample. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (48.8%) and top-target concentration (14.4%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample (52.7% and 16.1%).

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: not fully consistent - one real missing-enchant gap (Back) plus four slots (Head, Waist, Trinket2, Back) flagged for review as real, unexplained differences across kills rather than an obvious benign swap
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Back (item 29989) carries no permanent enchant across all 9 kills - the same real gap seen in both of Vinnyvozz's other analyzed raid nights. Unlike those other nights, this report's own gear diff flags Head, Waist, Trinket2, and Back as differing across kills in a way it can't read as an obvious single-kill swap - worth a closer manual look at which specific kills each slot changed on, since this pipeline's own benign-swap heuristic didn't resolve it automatically here. Consumables (battle and guardian elixir) were complete on all 9 kills; food was the one exception, inactive on Morogrim specifically. Flask was never active on any of the 9 kills, though this pipeline's own consumable-completeness check doesn't require a flask to count a kill as complete.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: 9 of 10 real bosses attempted were killed this raid - Kael'thas Sunstrider was wiped 4 times and never downed. Overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse on all 9 kills that were completed, ranging from 6.4 points past the worst (Void Reaver) to 16.1 points past it (Karathress) - the same consistent pattern already seen in Vinnyvozz's other analyzed raid nights. The dominant single event of this raid was on The Lurker Below: Vinnyvozz died 105 seconds into a 334-second kill (31.5% of the way through), which explains that kill's 3rd-percentile score, its 31.5% active time, and the cluster of raid deaths in the back half of that fight after her healing had already stopped. Hydross the Unstable also stood out with 15 deaths, unusually high for that boss relative to Vinnyvozz's other two analyzed Hydross kills (0 deaths each). Rebirth was used twice this raid (Morogrim, targeting Porgrok; Alar, targeting Rolopally), both real, correctly-timed battle-rezzes rather than missed opportunities. The three weakest kills by percentile were Lurker (3rd, driven by the early death above), Karathress (17th), and Lady Vashj (19th). One real gear note: the Back slot carries no permanent enchant, and four slots (Head, Waist, Trinket2, Back) show real cross-kill differences this pipeline couldn't automatically confirm as benign.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Vinnyvozz died 105 seconds into The Lurker Below (31.5% of the way through a 334-second kill), which drove that kill to the 3rd percentile and a cluster of raid deaths in the back half of the fight once her healing had stopped. Kael'thas Sunstrider was also wiped 4 times and never killed this raid (9 of 10 real bosses downed).
